Summary: Implemented a reusable top-of-screen notification system in the Flutter frontend and migrated snackbar callsites to it. The app now mounts a global notification host from WAH4PApp, notifications slide down as neutral cards, and screens use AppNotificationCenter instead of ScaffoldMessenger snackbars. I also added a focused widget test for the new host and updated the sync-records screen test harness to include the notification host and dismiss the shared timer state.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Maintain the new notification host and service in the app root as the single source of truth for transient in-app messages.
2. [ongoing] Review any remaining dirty repository tests or unrelated warnings separately; they are not part of the notification migration scope.

Affected files:
1. [created] frontend/lib/app/app_notification_center.dart — Defines the reusable global notification service, message model, severity helpers, and auto-dismiss lifecycle for the new top notification system. | Notes: Singleton service used from feature screens and tests.
2. [created] frontend/lib/core/widgets/feature/app_notification_host.dart — Provides the reusable top-of-screen notification host/card UI with slide-down animation and neutral styling. | Notes: Mounted in the app root and exercised by a focused widget test.
3. [modified] frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart — Hosts the notification overlay at the MaterialApp level so messages are visible across the whole app. | Notes: Uses MaterialApp.builder to stack the host above routed content.
4. [modified] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/screens/appointment_booking_screen.dart — Migrated local snackbar helper calls to the shared notification service. | Notes: Uses info severity for the booking flow prompts.
5. [modified] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/presentation/screens/sync_records_wizard_screen.dart — Migrated all wizard prompts and backend feedback to the shared notification service. | Notes: Keeps the screen behavior but routes messages through AppNotificationCenter.
6. [modified] frontend/lib/features/phr/blood_pressure/presentation/screen/blood_pressure_screen.dart — Replaced direct snackbar usage with shared warning/success/error notifications. | Notes: Removed the now-unused local snackbar helper.
7. [modified] frontend/lib/features/phr/body_mass_index/presentation/screen/body_mass_index_screen.dart — Replaced BMI validation and result snackbars with shared warning/error notifications. | Notes: Keeps user feedback consistent with the new notification surface.
8. [modified] frontend/lib/features/phr/medicine_intake/presentation/screen/medicine_intake_screen.dart — Replaced medicine intake snackbar feedback with the shared notification service. | Notes: Removed the now-unused local snackbar helper.
9. [modified] frontend/lib/features/phr/medicine_intake/presentation/widgets/medicine_intake_add_dialog.dart — Converted dialog validation snackbar to the shared warning notification. | Notes: Dialog now uses the same notification system as full screens.

content:
Summary: Planned a repo-wide migration from ScaffoldMessenger snackbars to a reusable top-of-screen notification card in the Flutter frontend. Discovery showed about 57 snackbar callsites across 21 files, mostly in auth, PHR, profile, interoperability, and appointments screens plus one dialog. The proposed design is a shared notification banner component plus a global controller/service mounted at the app root so notifications can slide down from the top and work from any screen or dialog.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Design the shared notification widget, controller/service, and app-root host so notifications can be triggered globally and dismissed/queued safely.
2. [ongoing] Replace every direct SnackBar/ScaffoldMessenger snackbar callsite in the frontend with the new reusable notification API.
3. [ongoing] Add focused widget tests for the notification card/host and verify the migrated screens still render and trigger notifications correctly.

Affected files:
1. [inspected] frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart — This is the MaterialApp entrypoint and likely host for the global notification system. | Notes: Contains the existing navigator key and theme setup.
2. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/constants/app_colors.dart — Needed to understand the current palette and avoid introducing conflicting notification colors. | Notes: Current primary color is purple, but the new notification should not rely on purple tint.
3. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/constants/app_border_radii.dart — Needed to match the existing rounded card language for the notification surface. | Notes: All shared radii resolve to a 20px rounded style.
4. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/constants/app_text_styles.dart — Needed to keep typography consistent inside the notification card. | Notes: Provides the reusable body/title styles used across shared UI.
5. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/widgets/feature/app_bottom_sheet_widget.dart — Shows existing shared surface composition patterns for reusable feature-level widgets. | Notes: Useful styling reference for a new notification card/host.
6. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/widgets/ui/buttons/primary_button_widget.dart — Shows shared widget organization and styling conventions for reusable UI primitives. | Notes: Confirms the repo favors explicit reusable UI components.
7. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/widgets/ui/inputs/bottom_sheet_select_form_field.dart — Shows how shared widgets are structured and how overlays are used in the codebase. | Notes: Useful reference for building a reusable notification host or overlay behavior.
8. [inspected] frontend/lib/features/calendar/presentation/widgets/calendar_view_mode_dropdown.dart — Contains an existing OverlayEntry pattern that can inform the new notification animation/placement approach. | Notes: Demonstrates the codebase already uses overlays for floating UI.
9. [inspected] frontend/lib/features/health_records/presentation/widgets/health_record_filter_dropdown.dart — Provides another overlay pattern and cleanup behavior example for floating UI components. | Notes: Useful for lifecycle and dismissal handling.
10. [inspected] frontend/pubspec.yaml — Needed to confirm available dependencies and avoid planning around a package that is not present. | Notes: No extra animation package is currently declared.

Summary: Implemented the next round of APC testing fixes for the auth/profile cluster. UAT-09 was clarified with explicit name guidance in registration and profile forms, the email verification resend cooldown was increased, MPIN confirmation now returns the user to setup when the code mismatches, and MPIN login now offers a recovery path into password reset. The fix-status checklist in for-fixing.md was updated to mark UAT-09 as fixed while keeping broader MPIN/recovery items open.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Follow up on UAT-11 registration persistence, complete MPIN reset behavior, and any remaining verification-timing or back-navigation gaps.
2. [ongoing] Investigate UAT-12 login/MPIN state transitions if testers can still reproduce a stuck login path.
3. [ongoing] Plan a dedicated MPIN reset flow if the password-reset fallback is not sufficient for the UAT-13 expectation.
4. [ongoing] Continue the separate interoperability/sync workstream for UAT-15 to UAT-20 without changing current sync logic in place.

Summary: Implemented a first pass of UI-only fixes from the APC testing comments while explicitly avoiding sync/interoperability changes. Updated splash branding, added stronger password validation and a live password mismatch notice, improved the personal-information birth-date field with a date picker and MM/DD/YYYY display, and made the dashboard profile-completion prompt dismiss reliably. Added targeted tests for the new splash branding, password validator, and adjusted profile screen expectations. Verified the updated frontend tests passed.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Continue the remaining UI defect backlog if the user wants more APC comments addressed, starting with dashboard/calendar readability and other non-sync frontend issues.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_validators.dart — Expanded password policy to require a special character and surfaced that requirement in validation responses and requirement lists. | Notes: This is part of the password-registration UI hardening.

6. [modified] frontend/test/widget_test.dart — Updated the startup test to match the actual startup gate used by the app. | Notes: The test now checks the startup gate text instead of the splash widget.
7. [modified] frontend/test/features/profile/presentation/screens/personal_information_screen_test.dart — Adjusted profile-screen expectations to match the updated birth-date label and optional indicator wording. | Notes: Keeps the UI test aligned with the new form copy.

content:
Summary: Fixed the sync simulation failure that surfaced as 'unable to store observation record' by making the backend observation/lab record filter text length-safe before insert. The root cause was an overlong laboratory_result_records.filter_value exceeding the database's 80-character check constraint. Added a reusable filter builder in the FHIR sync mapper so clinical record filter values are bounded, and added regression coverage for the PH Core parsers to assert filterValue length stays within 80 characters. Verified with targeted backend tests, backend build, and the sync simulation e2e route test.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Confirm the sync simulation no longer throws the observation storage error in the authenticated flow.
2. [finished] Preserve the bearer-token-based simulation path and avoid reintroducing public profileId exposure.
3. [finished] Keep regression coverage for PH Core clinical record parsing and storage constraints.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.mapper.ts — Added the length-safe filter builder and updated clinical record mapping so inserted filter values cannot exceed the database constraint. | Notes: This is the primary production fix for the observation storage failure.
2. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/fhir-parser.registry.spec.ts — Added regression coverage asserting clinical record filter values stay within the 80-character database limit. | Notes: Covers the observation fixture path that previously failed.
3. [inspected] backend/package.json — Confirmed the available backend test and build scripts before running validation. | Notes: No file changes were needed.
4. [inspected] sqls/007_health_records_setup.sql — Confirmed the laboratory_result_records schema and its 80-character filter_value constraint to identify the root cause. | Notes: Read-only inspection only.
5. [inspected] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.service.ts — Verified the persistence flow that inserts parsed clinical resources into the repository. | Notes: No code change required in this file.

Summary: Fixed the sync simulation auth-context problem by switching the frontend to send the authenticated Supabase access token as a Bearer Authorization header, and by making the backend simulation route resolve the current user from that token through AuthSupportService, with x-user-id only as a fallback. The request body no longer carries profileId, and the simulation still persists the PH Core bundle against the authenticated account via the existing receive-results-for-profile path. Validation passed after the change.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Continue to use bearer-token authentication for the simulation route instead of depending on profileId in request bodies.
2. [ongoing] If future auth-context bugs appear, verify AuthSession.accessToken is populated before invoking the simulation action.

1. [inspected] backend/src/auth/auth.module.ts — Confirmed AuthSupportService is exported so the integration module can reuse existing Supabase-user resolution. | Notes: No code change required here.
2. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.module.ts — Imported AuthModule so the simulation service can resolve the authenticated user from the access token. | Notes: Keeps auth resolution reusable rather than duplicating it.
3. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.controller.ts — Changed the simulation route to accept Authorization and optional x-user-id headers and pass them to the simulation service. | Notes: The route now supports bearer-token auth context.
4. [modified] backend/src/integration/sync-simulation.service.ts — Resolved profile ID from the bearer token using AuthSupportService, with x-user-id fallback, before persisting the simulated PH Core bundle. | Notes: This is the core runtime fix for the missing account context error.
5. [modified] backend/src/integration/dto/simulate-sync-request.dto.ts — Removed profileId from the public simulation DTO so the body only contains request data relevant to the sync action. | Notes: Prevents users from having to provide internal UUIDs.
6. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.types.ts — Removed the obsolete public simulation payload type that exposed profileId. | Notes: Keeps the contract aligned with the new auth-based flow.
7. [modified] backend/test/app.e2e-spec.ts — Updated the simulation e2e test to use a bearer Authorization header and mocked AuthSupportService account resolution. | Notes: Verifies the real auth-context path instead of the fallback header.
8. [modified] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/data/interoperability_api_client.dart — Changed the simulation client to send Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and to support extra request headers in POST calls. | Notes: Matches the rest of the app’s authenticated API calls.

[modified] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/presentation/screens/sync_records_wizard_screen.dart — Switched the simulation action to use AuthSession.accessToken and show a clear sign-in prompt when the token is unavailable. | Notes: The button now uses the authenticated session token rather than the user ID.

content:
Summary: Adjusted the sync simulation flow so the user-facing request no longer carries a profileId in the body. The frontend now sends the authenticated account ID in an internal x-user-id header, while the backend simulation endpoint resolves the profile context from that header and persists the generated PH Core bundle through the existing receive-results-for-profile path. This preserves UUID-based internal storage without exposing profileId to the user and resolved the validation error the user was seeing.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Confirm the simulation flow compiles and passes backend e2e plus Flutter analysis after removing the body-level profileId.
2. [ongoing] Keep any future account-context propagation on internal headers or session-aware mechanisms rather than exposing profileId in the request body.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/integration/dto/simulate-sync-request.dto.ts — Removed profileId from the public simulation request DTO so the request body no longer requires a UUID field from the user. | Notes: This is the main API-shape fix.
2. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.controller.ts — Changed the simulation route to read the internal x-user-id header and pass it to the simulation service. | Notes: Keeps account context internal to the transport layer.
3. [modified] backend/src/integration/sync-simulation.service.ts — Updated the simulation service to require a header-derived user ID and persist the simulated bundle against that profile context. | Notes: No longer depends on a body-level profileId.
4. [modified] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/data/interoperability_api_client.dart — Changed the simulation client to send x-user-id as a header instead of profileId in the body, and added header merging support to the POST helper. | Notes: The body now matches the user-facing request shape.
5. [modified] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/presentation/screens/sync_records_wizard_screen.dart — Updated the wizard to pass the signed-in session user ID into the simulation client and keep the sign-in guard for missing context. | Notes: The button stays hidden from UUID details.
6. [modified] backend/test/app.e2e-spec.ts — Updated the e2e simulation request to send x-user-id and removed the profileId body field. | Notes: Verifies the new transport shape.
7. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.types.ts — Removed the obsolete simulation payload type that exposed profileId in the public contract. | Notes: Prevents stale typing from reintroducing the field.

Summary: Implemented a new interoperability simulation flow so the Flutter sync wizard can post real PH Core example data into the backend and store it against the signed-in account/profile. The backend now exposes a simulated sync endpoint that reuses the existing prepare flow for validation, loads checked-in PH Core fixtures, and forwards a Bundle into the existing FHIR receive-results persistence path. The frontend gained a neutral secondary Simulate request button under Prepare request, plus request/result plumbing and updated review copy. Validation passed with backend e2e coverage and Flutter analysis.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Complete the new backend simulation endpoint wiring and fixture loading logic for PH Core sample data.

content:
Summary: Implemented a strict PH Core-aware FHIR inbound parsing layer for the backend webhook flow. The service now validates inbound receive-results and receive-push payloads through per-resource parser modules before mapping and persisting data, and the shared mapper was improved to handle array-shaped encounter code fields from PH Core examples. Added fixture-driven parser and service tests, and verified the backend e2e suite still passes.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Implement the next phase of simulated POST tests that send PH Core webhook payloads through the backend routes end-to-end.
2. [ongoing] Consider expanding the parsers if additional PH Core resource families or stricter field-level validation rules are needed later.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.service.ts — Wired inbound webhook handling through the new parser registry before persistence, keeping route handling thin and validating PH Core payloads first. | Notes: receiveResults and receivePush now parse resources before repository writes.
2. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.mapper.ts — Improved shared mapping logic for array-shaped codeable-concept fields so PH Core encounter payloads normalize correctly. | Notes: Encounter titles now resolve from array-shaped type fields.
3. [created] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.profile.ts — Centralized strict PH Core profile suffix rules for supported FHIR resource families. | Notes: Used by parser modules to validate declared profiles.
4. [created] backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/fhir-parser.types.ts — Defined the discriminated union types for parsed inbound patient and clinical resources. | Notes: Keeps parser outputs explicit and typed.
5. [created] backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/fhir-parser.utils.ts — Added shared parser boundary helpers for resource-type validation and PH Core profile detection. | Notes: Shared by all resource-specific parsers.
6. [created] backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/clinical.parser.ts — Implemented the generic clinical resource parser that validates and normalizes supported non-patient FHIR payloads. | Notes: Delegates resource-specific mapping to the existing mapper.
7. [created] backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/patient.parser.ts — Implemented the PH Core patient parser boundary. | Notes: Returns the validated Patient resource for profile updates and identifier matching.

Summary: Fixed the PowerShell FHIR seed script so it no longer fails on duplicate profile primary keys. The seed flow now treats the profile created by the auth trigger as an upsert target and also upserts patient_identifiers, making reruns idempotent after partial attempts. The script still seeds PH Core example bundles into /fhir/receive-results for immunization, consultation history, medical history, and laboratory results.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Optionally add a README note clarifying that the seed script creates or updates the profile row via upsert.

content:
Summary: Removed PhilSys ID from the backend profile contract and aligned the frontend shared profile model with the trimmed response shape. The backend now no longer validates, stores, returns, or includes philSysId in auth/profile flows, and a SQL migration was added to strip existing philSysId keys from profiles.patient_profile JSONB rows. The frontend profile model and tests were updated to remove the stale field, while the Personal Information screen continues to rely on PhilHealth ID only. Backend build and targeted frontend Flutter tests passed after the change.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Remove philSysId from backend DTOs, profile service, repository mapping, and login fallback response objects.
2. [finished] Add a SQL migration to strip philSysId from existing patient_profile JSONB rows.
3. [finished] Update the frontend shared profile model and related tests to remove the stale philSysId field.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/auth/auth.types.ts — Removed philSysId from the backend patient profile response contract so the API no longer exposes the field. | Notes: This is the canonical backend response shape used by login/profile flows.

3. [modified] backend/src/auth/profile.service.ts — Removed philSysId from the profile-to-JSON mapping used when saving profile updates and creating empty profile payloads. | Notes: This keeps backend writes aligned with the reduced contract.

Summary: Reviewed the frontend Health Records screens and the existing Personal Records architecture to plan a future local persistence layer for Health Records. The current Health Records feature is still presentation-driven with hardcoded entry data in the screen files, while Personal Records already has a dedicated typed data/repository pattern. The recommended approach is to introduce a separate local health-records repository and domain model layer so the UI can read from a single source of truth now and later support creation without reworking the screens.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Design the Health Records local data model, repository API, and storage boundary so current screens can read seeded records now and accept created records later.
2. [ongoing] Identify the minimal set of frontend files that would need to switch from hardcoded entries to repository-backed data loading when implementation starts.

Summary: Added a plain-English atlas for WAH4P so future work can read the project like a map. The repo is best understood in layers: a Flutter client that controls navigation and presentation, a NestJS backend that owns security and business rules, and Supabase/Postgres plus secure local storage for persistence. The most important user journeys now have an explicit flow: splash/startup -> onboarding or login -> dashboard or MPIN unlock -> feature areas like records, appointments, resupply, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. This memory also clarifies which screens are static, which are route-driven, and which backend APIs support each major journey.

Tasks:

2. [ongoing] Reconcile older route documentation with the live Flutter route tree when updating screen inventories or onboarding future contributors.
3. [ongoing] Use the layer model in future work plans: start from frontend flow, then check backend API support, then verify data persistence rules.

metadata.summary: Added a plain-English atlas for WAH4P so future work can read the project like a map. The repo is best understood in layers: a Flutter client that controls navigation and presentation, a NestJS backend that owns security and business rules, and Supabase/Postgres plus secure local storage for persistence. The most important user journeys now have an explicit flow: splash/startup -> onboarding or login -> dashboard or MPIN unlock -> feature areas like records, appointments, resupply, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. This memory also clarifies which screens are static, which are route-driven, and which backend APIs support each major journey.

content:
Summary: Audited the WAH4P repository and saved a clear project snapshot for future work. The repo is split into a NestJS backend, a Flutter frontend, and supporting SQL/docs. The backend is a secured API with global API-key protection, throttling, strict env validation, Supabase auth/data integration, and Resend email delivery. The frontend is a Flutter mobile app with centralized theming, secure local storage, a startup gate, and MPIN/session lifecycle handling. The docs describe a Supabase/Postgres source of truth for patient data, with frontend screens mostly acting as presentation and interaction layers. I also captured the current testing surface and a couple of documentation mismatches that future work should reconcile.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Reconcile SCREENS.md and related docs with the current frontend tree so the route inventory matches the live Flutter codebase.
2. [ongoing] Continue enforcing the backend env contract so every required secret and origin value is provided in .env.example and validated at startup.
3. [ongoing] Use the existing backend and frontend test scaffolding as the baseline for future changes, and expand tests where new screen or auth flows are added.
